name: Nacimiento del río Pedrajas, Alto De La Risca y Los Castillejos – circular desde Cidones
region_intro: Hiking around Cidones offers a diverse landscape characterized by forested hills, natural springs, and river valleys. The region features a mix of gentle terrain suitable for easy walks and more challenging paths leading to elevated viewpoints. Hikers can explore areas with varied elevations, including modest peaks and tranquil lakeside trails. Best hiking trails around Cidones The most popular hiking route is Pico Frentes and La Toba Waterfall Loop Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a peak and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa Pita Loop Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This circular route explores the area around Playa Pita, offering lakeside scenery. Local hikers also love the Abejar Springs and Viewpoints Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through natural springs and scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Cidones is defined by forested hills, natural springs, and varied viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Cidones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Nearly 200 visitors have used komoot to explore Cidones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Cidones?
A: Yes, Cidones offers 12 easy hiking trails. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379179877/playa-pita-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) circular route that explores the scenic lakeside area.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Cidones?
A: Hiking around Cidones features diverse natural landscapes including forested hills, natural springs, and river valleys. You can find routes that lead to waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058987989/pico-frentes-and-la-toba-waterfall-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Frentes and La Toba Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas with natural springs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063217868/abejar-springs-and-viewpoints-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejar Springs and Viewpoints Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cidones?
A: Many of the easy trails in Cidones are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063069392/pinturas-rupestres-canada-honda-y-mirador-la-cabana-circular-por-el-monte-valonsadero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinturas rupestres, Cañada Honda y Mirador La Cabaña – circular por el Monte Valonsadero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.6-mile (4.1 km) loop that offers interesting sights like rock art, making it an engaging option for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cidones?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Cidones, especially in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas. The region's varied terrain offers plenty of opportunities for walks with your canine companion.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Cidones?
A: Yes, Cidones offers several excellent circular hiking routes. Besides the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379179877/playa-pita-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can also enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063165785/nacimiento-del-rio-pedrajas-alto-de-la-risca-y-los-castillejos-circular-desde-cidones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Pedrajas, Alto De La Risca y Los Castillejos – circular desde Cidones&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) loop that explores the source of the Pedrajas river and offers elevated views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cidones?
A: The best time for hiking in Cidones is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant with wildflowers or autumn colors. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter hiking is possible, but some trails might be muddy or have snow at higher elevations.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Cidones's forested hills and natural areas are home to various wildlife. Hikers might spot deer, wild boar, and a variety of bird species. Keep an eye out for smaller mammals and reptiles. Respecting their habitat by staying on marked trails and keeping noise to a minimum increases your chances of a sighting.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest to see near the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely! Beyond the trails themselves, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Honda Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into ancient history. For natural beauty, visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2999663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Toba&lt;/a&gt; waterfall or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4077489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Golmayo River&lt;/a&gt;. There are also several viewpoints and peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Frentes&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic vistas.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Cidones?
A: Cidones offers a comprehensive network of over 30 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. This includes 12 easy routes, 21 moderate trails, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cidones?
A: The hiking routes in Cidones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from forested hills and natural springs to scenic viewpoints and tranquil lakeside paths, as well as the well-maintained trails suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking routes in Cidones?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail might be limited, Cidones and its surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals. It's a good idea to check local listings or plan your route to pass through a village for a break. Packing snacks and water is always recommended for longer hikes.
tips: Nice painting next to the Pedrajas river that leads us to its source about 500 meters away.

It is the highest peak of the Sierra de Cabrejas having 1433 meters.
From its summit there is a spectacular view from the Cuerda del Pozo reservoir to Soria to the east.
Going up is not very complicated, I went up the Viñuela path to Los Castillejos and from there I went up a narrow pass to the top.

It is a pelendón Celtiberian town from between the 3rd and 2nd centuries BC located on a rocky outcrop.
It is surrounded by ravines on 3 of its sides while on the south, which was the most accessible part, a wall was built of which up to 5 meters high, 2 towers and 2 gates are preserved.
Some houses attached to the wall have also been excavated in which ceramics and various Celtiberian utensils have been found.
Access is difficult as it is on a high ledge and you can only see the remains of what was once the wall but it is worth going to see it in addition to the views.
the best option is to go up from Ocenilla along the Viñuela road

waypoints.name: Pine Forest by the Pedrajas River
